nodes.speaker: Logic
nodes.text: I isolate patterns. Causes, consequences, contradictions. Call me Logic if a name helps.

nodes.speaker: Empathy
nodes.text: I feel what the others reduce to data: pain, fear, the weight of another person's life. I am Empathy.

nodes.speaker: Scepticism
nodes.text: Names are convenient. Evidence is better. I doubt what has not earned belief. Scepticism will do.

nodes.speaker: Memory
nodes.text: I keep what remains. Faces without names. Warnings without endings. I think I was Memory.

nodes.speaker: Conscience
nodes.text: And I ask what your choices will cost. Not to condemn you. To ensure the cost is seen. I am Conscience.

nodes.speaker: Logic
nodes.text: Incorrect. Separate functions, one injured consciousness. The distinction may become important.

nodes.speaker: System
nodes.text: Investigation available. Inspect the damaged device and at least two other objects before leaving.

nodes.speaker: Conscience
nodes.text: The accident did not end inside that chamber. Something crossed with you, and consequences may already be moving.

nodes.speaker: Empathy
nodes.text: You do not need every answer before you stand. You need one reliable next step.

nodes.speaker: Scepticism
nodes.text: Reliable means tested. The memory explains the damage, but the date must come from outside your head.

nodes.speaker: Memory
nodes.text: The instruction is still missing. Perhaps it waits beyond the room. Perhaps I lost it on the way here.

nodes.speaker: Logic
nodes.text: Priority order: confirm displacement, establish chronology, then determine whether repair is possible.

nodes.speaker: System
nodes.text: Choose which part of your consciousness you will rely on first.
